owner: Vasile Lucian Borbeleac / FRAGMERGENT TECHNOLOGY S.R.L.
vision: Dual-agent, memory-native transformer (D_Cortex) where two separate neural networks (Encoder/Agent A writes facts to memory banks; Decoder/Agent B reads memory and produces language) meet ONLY through shared memory banks, with no weight sharing and no gradient leak between agents.
subproject_objective: Bring up a REAL local single-GPU training run of DCortexV2Model on a Windows 11 + NVIDIA RTX 5080 (Blackwell, sm_120) machine, adapted from the Colab reference colab/step2_training_v11.py. Deliver a working run with genuinely decreasing training loss and resumable atomic checkpoints in one pass.
core_goals: Reproduce the v11 'Objective B1: Complex Episodes' training locally without Google Colab/Drive.
Use bf16 autocast with NO GradScaler (Blackwell behaves like A100 here).
Verify four pre-declared PASS/FAIL gates on real measured numbers: ENV, FIT (<15GB peak VRAM), LEARNS (>=15% train-loss drop over 300 steps), RESUME (checkpoint continuity).

platform.gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080, 16 GB GDDR7, Blackwell GB203, compute capability sm_120 (12.0)
platform.torch_requirement: torch>=2.7 built for CUDA 12.8 (cu128) - older cu121/cu124 fail with 'no kernel image available' on sm_120

key_constraints: DO NOT modify the dcortex/ model architecture or any file under dcortex/.
Precision: bf16 autocast, NO GradScaler. Do not use the fp16+GradScaler path.
16 GB VRAM: gradient checkpointing on, reduced batch, peak VRAM target < 15 GB.
No synthetic LM data: if dataset acquisition fails, raise RuntimeError with instructions.
Atomic checkpoints (.tmp then os.rename), full content, numeric-sort resume.
All code/comments/filenames in English, no em dashes, copyright header on new standalone .py.

dataset.structural_episodes: Synthetic entity->color fact episodes (simple/update/distractor), generated in-process. Independent of any corpus.
dataset.lm_corpus: Local E:\DATA\training_data_clean BYON jsonl files ({"text": ...} schema), tokenized to .bin via the same pipeline the repo used for TinyStories. Chosen by user on 2026-06-15 over the repo default (TinyStories) to honor the local data path and enable fully offline operation.
